description: Analysis Concept model with three levels: Statistical Concepts (atoms), Result Patterns (molecules), and Categories (groupings). The shared statistics vocabulary is referenced by both methods and result patterns but owned by neither.
designPrinciples: Statistical concepts are individual data points — each has its own identity in the graph
Result patterns group statistical concepts that belong together and should be interpreted together
Categories group result patterns that typically appear together in the same kind of analysis
The shared statistics vocabulary is independent — methods reference it for output slots, result patterns reference it for grouping
Methods do not reference AC concepts — the Transformation maps method output slots to result patterns
The model is representation-neutral — it works in tabular, graph, or any other physical format

qualifierTypes.note: Qualifier types are defined in the CDDM shared vocabulary (CDDM_Shared_QualifierTypes.json). They represent semantically different versions of a data definition. Statistical concepts and result patterns do not have qualifiers — qualifiers apply only to data definitions at the transformation/CDDM layer.

sharedStatisticsVocabulary.note: These are Level 1 Statistical Concepts. Both methods and result patterns reference them. Neither owns them.

CI_Lower.definition: Lower bound of a confidence interval

CI_Upper.definition: Upper bound of a confidence interval

resultPatterns.note: Level 2 Result Patterns. Each groups a set of statistical concepts and defines the identifying relationships for each instance.

LSMeans.definition: Least-squares means (adjusted marginal means) per factor level or level combination

Contrasts.definition: Pairwise or custom contrasts between LS means

Type3Tests.definition: Type III omnibus tests, one per model term

ParameterEstimates.definition: Solution for fixed effects, one row per model coefficient

SurvivalTable.definition: Life table with survival estimates at each event time

EventSummary.definition: Summary counts of events and censoring per group

categories.note: Level 3 AC Categories. Group result patterns that typically appear together.

TreatmentComparison.definition: A comparison of an outcome between treatment groups, producing estimated treatment effects with inference.

SurvivalAnalysis.definition: An analysis of time-to-event outcomes, estimating survival patterns and comparing them between groups.

DescriptiveSummary.definition: A statistical summary of a distribution within a group. No comparison between groups required.

methodOutputSlotMapping.note: This shows how method output slots map to result patterns. The method defines slots using the shared vocabulary. The Transformation maps slots to result patterns. Neither the method nor the result pattern references the other directly.
